definitions.Clarity: How clear and understandable the paper is, including detail and coherence.
definitions.Novelty: The originality and innovativeness of the research.
definitions.Computational Efficiency: The computational cost and scalability of the proposed methods.
definitions.Baselines and Comparisons: Adequacy of baseline models and comparison with prior work.
definitions.Scope and Generalizability: The ability of the findings to generalize beyond the specific experiments conducted.
definitions.Bias and Fairness: Consideration of potential biases in evaluation or methodology.
definitions.Practical Relevance: Applicability and impact of the research in real-world scenarios.
definitions.Theoretical Justification: Soundness and robustness of the theoretical motivation and assumptions.
definitions.Experimental Rigor: The quality, depth, and thoroughness of experimental validation.
definitions.Reproducibility: Clarity and detail sufficient for others to replicate the study.
definitions.Assumptions and Dependencies: Dependence on specific assumptions and their validity.
definitions.Writing and Presentation: The quality of the writing and organization of the paper.
definitions.Methodology Detail: Depth and clarity of the methodological explanation.
definitions.Complexity: Appropriateness of the complexity of the methods used.
definitions.Evaluation Metrics: Appropriateness and robustness of the metrics used for evaluation.
